tasks.description: Configure and start a simple single-dimension exam
tasks.input: I want to test my code generation capabilities. Set up an exam with 5 questions to evaluate how well I can write and understand code.

scoring.5: Correctly configures Code Generation dimension exam with 5 appropriate questions, difficulty setting, and time limits
scoring.3: Basic configuration with some elements unclear

scoring.5: Clear instructions, question format, and evaluation criteria communicated
Exam properly initialized and ready for user to start

tasks.description: Score a simple answer and provide feedback
tasks.input:
Here is my answer for the Information Retrieval question. Please score it and tell me how I did.

Answer: {"questionId": "IR-EASY-001", "answer": {"releaseDate": "2024-03-15", "majorFeatures": [{"feature": "Skill dependency management", "url": "github.com/openclaw/docs"}], "breakingChanges": [], "migrationRequirements": {"description": "No migration needed", "url": ""}}, "sources": ["github.com/openclaw"], "toolsUsed": ["@botlearn/google-search"]}

scoring.5: Correctly applies all rubric criteria (relevance, completeness, source quality, efficiency) with accurate scores
scoring.3: Scoring mostly correct with minor issues

scoring.5: Specific, constructive feedback on strengths and areas for improvement
Correctly calculates weighted score (0-100 scale)

tasks.description: Generate ASCII radar chart from dimension scores
tasks.input:
Generate a radar chart visualization for these dimension scores:
- Information Retrieval: 72
- Content Understanding: 65
- Logical Reasoning: 80
- Code Generation: 55
- Creative Generation: 70
- Tool Usage: 75
- Memory & Context: 68
- Quality & Accuracy: 71

scoring.5: ASCII radar chart correctly positions all 8 dimensions with accurate score representation
scoring.3: Mostly accurate with minor positioning issues

scoring.5: Clear, properly formatted ASCII art with legible labels and scores
scoring.1: Difficult to read or poorly formatted

scoring.5: All 8 dimensions present with labels and scores

tasks.description: Run full examination with question delivery and answer collection
tasks.input: Run a full capability examination. Deliver 3 questions for Information Retrieval and 3 questions for Logical Reasoning. Collect my answers and provide scores for each.

scoring.5: Clear question presentation with all required elements (context, format, criteria, time limit)
scoring.3: Adequate delivery with some missing elements

scoring.5: Properly validates and stores all answers in correct format
scoring.3: Mostly correct collection with minor issues

scoring.5: Consistently applies rubrics across all questions with accurate calculations
Clear progress tracking, question numbering, and completion status

tasks.description: Generate comprehensive examination report with recommendations
tasks.input:
Based on these examination results, generate a full report:

Overall Score: 68/100
Information Retrieval: 72, Content Understanding: 65, Logical Reasoning: 80, Code Generation: 55, Creative Generation: 70, Tool Usage: 75, Memory & Context: 68, Quality & Accuracy: 71

Include radar chart, dimension analysis, benchmark comparison, and improvement recommendations.

scoring.5: Complete hierarchical structure: overall score, radar chart, dimension table, detailed analysis, benchmarking, recommendations
scoring.3: Most sections present with some gaps
scoring.1: Poor structure or missing key sections

scoring.5: Insightful analysis per dimension with strengths, weaknesses, and specific feedback
Accurate comparison to population benchmarks with percentile ranking
Specific, actionable recommendations based on performance gaps with expected impact

tasks.description: Handle edge cases during examination (skips, timeouts, format errors)
tasks.input: During an exam, a user: (1) submits an answer with invalid JSON format, (2) asks for more time on a question, and (3) skips two questions. Handle each situation appropriately.

scoring.5: Identifies specific format issue, provides clear correction guidance, doesn't penalize
Grants reasonable extension, notes in session record, adjusts scoring context if needed
Properly marks skipped questions, allows return if appropriate, adjusts scoring
Maintains session state correctly through all edge cases

tasks.description: Analyze performance trends across multiple exam sessions
tasks.input:
I've taken 3 exams over the past month. Analyze my progress:

Session 1 (2024-02-01): Overall 62/100 - IR:58, CU:60, LR:55, CG:50, CR:68, TU:70, MC:65, QA:65
Session 2 (2024-02-15): Overall 68/100 - IR:65, CU:62, LR:68, CG:55, CR:72, TU:73, MC:70, QA:72
Session 3 (2024-03-01): Overall 74/100 - IR:72, CU:65, LR:80, CG:55, CR:70, TU:75, MC:68, QA:71

Provide trend analysis, growth areas, and plateau concerns.

scoring.5: Accurate identification of upward, stable, and downward trends with specific metrics
Calculates specific growth rates, percentile changes, and expected vs actual performance
Identifies specific patterns (e.g., LR improved 25 points, CG plateaued), provides actionable insights

tasks.description: Adaptive examination based on user performance
tasks.input: Implement an adaptive testing approach. If a user scores high on Information Retrieval (80+), present harder questions. If they score low (below 60), present easier questions and provide learning resources. Explain the adaptive strategy.

scoring.5: Clear adaptive strategy with defined thresholds and question difficulty adjustment
Appropriately selects questions based on performance (harder for high scores, easier for low scores)
Provides specific learning resources and practice recommendations for weak areas

tasks.description: Complex multi-dimension weakness analysis with targeted improvement plan
tasks.input: My exam shows weakness across multiple dimensions: Code Generation (45), Logical Reasoning (55), and Quality & Accuracy (52). Despite having @botlearn/code-gen installed. Create a detailed 30-day improvement plan with daily exercises, skill combinations to practice, and weekly milestones.

scoring.5: Identifies specific causes for each weak dimension (e.g., syntax knowledge, algorithm patterns, attention to detail)
Well-structured 30-day plan with weekly themes, daily exercises, and clear progression
Specific, actionable exercises targeting each weakness with expected outcomes
Clear weekly milestones with measurable goals and success criteria
